Lesson Content: Introduction to 3D Vectors: position vectors, displacement vectors, magnitude
Vector Operations: addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, unit vectors
Vector Algebra Practice: points, vectors, parallel vectors, basic exam-style questions
Scalar Product 1: dot product, algebraic form, geometric meaning
Scalar Product 2: angle between vectors, perpendicular vectors, unknown parameters
Dot Product Applications: mixed practice and Chapter 9.1-9.3 consolidation
Vector Equation of a Line 1: point and direction vector, vector form
Vector Equation of a Line 2: parametric and Cartesian forms
Lines in 3D: point on a line, parallel lines, intersection basics
Line Relationships: intersecting, parallel, and skew lines
Vector Product 1: cross product calculation and perpendicular vectors
Vector Product 2: area of triangles/parallelograms and line applications
Vector Equation of a Plane 1: plane form and normal vectors
Vector Equation of a Plane 2: Cartesian equation and point-plane checks
Lines, Planes, and Angles: line-plane intersection and Chapter 9 review
Complex Numbers 1: real and imaginary parts, conjugates, Argand diagram
Complex Numbers 2: modulus, argument, quadrant checks
Complex Numbers 3: Cartesian form, polar form, modulus-argument form
Operations with Complex Numbers: multiplication and division in polar form
De Moivre's Theorem: powers of complex numbers
Roots of Complex Numbers: nth roots, Argand diagrams, Chapter 10 review
Probability Axioms: sample spaces, events, complements, unions and intersections
Conditional Probability: tree diagrams, Venn diagrams, P(A|B)
Independence and Mutually Exclusive Events: comparison and mixed practice
Discrete Random Variables: probability distributions, expected value
Variance and Standard Deviation: E(X), E(X^2), Var(X)
Continuous Random Variables: PDFs, area under the curve, integration practice
Binomial Distribution 1: binomial conditions, X~B(n,p), exact probability
Binomial Distribution 2: cumulative probability, complements, calculator use
Binomial Distribution 3: mean, variance, unknown parameters, exam-style practice
Normal Distribution 1: shape, mean, standard deviation, standardization
Normal Distribution 2: normal CDF, interval probabilities, tail probabilities
Normal Distribution 3: inverse normal, unknown mean or standard deviation, Chapter 11 review
Exploration Introduction: purpose, assessment focus, research question basics
Exploration Topic Workshop: modelling ideas from vectors, complex numbers, and probability
Final Consolidation: Chapters 9-11 mixed review and cumulative mini mock
